observations.recommendation: We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to prepare written guidelines prior to the inventory taking with clear instructions that all tangible assets are to be counted and to define roles of the members of the Inventory Committee to ensure the timely completion and submission of the RPCPPE.
observations.reason: Members of the inventory committee lack training on Property Management System (PSMS).

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Accountant to write demand letters signed by the Mayor for the immediate settlement of the cash advances; additional cash advance shall not be given until the previous cash advance is settled; cause the immediate withholding of salaries and claims of officers and employees who failed to settle their accounts; furnish all department heads of listings of unliquidated cash advances of employees to enhance efforts on the settlements.
observations.reason: Not all cash advances were liquidated

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal Accountant to appropriately disclose in the financial statements the details of the loan and safeguard document files of the loan for safety and future reference.
observations.reason: Loan documents were not furnished to Municipal Accounting Office

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to make disclosure on the dormant balances of the account Due from NGOs and other LGUs.
observations.reason: Debit balance cannot be traced due to lack of supporting documents

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal Accountant to request for the write-off of the dormant accounts with validation for identified the dead debtors, no pending cases, with unknown whereabouts and have exhausted all possible means for the collection.
observations.reason: Lack of supporting documents identified the dead debtors, no pending cases, unknown whereabouts and have exhausted all possible means for the collection

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal Engineer and the Bids and Awards Committee to implement immediately the projects under the 20% Development Fund and to prioritize the conduct of bidding over other activities pursuant to pertinent provisions of RIRR of RA No. 9184.
observations.reason: Not all 20% Development Fund projects are implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to direct the LDRRMO to implement programs of LDRRMP that are being monitored in order to efficiently respond to calamity thereby ensuring sustained recovery.
observations.reason: Current LDRRMF was not fully utilized

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the Municipal Mayor and the local sanggunian to: a) craft ordinances and issue executive orders relative to waste management and environmental protection; b) make current waste management system doable by enhancing garbage classification, collection, and recycling; c) add facilities to the landfill to improve recycling methods.
observations.reason: Implementation is still on-going.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to direct the GAD FPS to adhere to the rules and regulations on cost attribution of GAD, in order to address efficiently and effectively the streamlined gender issues.
observations.reason: Implementation is still on-going.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary disclosure on the dormant balances of NGA-BIR account.
observations.reason: Implementation is still on-going.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal Accountant make the necessary disclosure with the dormant balances of the account.
observations.reason: Implementation is still on-going.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Health Officer facilitate the licensing of the ambulance vehicles.
We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal Accountant to enforce stringent measures for the settlement of disallowances.
observations.reason: Settling of disallowances is on-going

observations.recommendation: We recommended that a proper gate at the entrance to the RCA must be built so that it is easier for the personnel at the RCA to inspect and direct the segregation of waste to the designated area and, implement appropriate means of identification to ensure that wastes are properly segregated at the RCA.
observations.reason: Lack of manpower

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Solid Waste Management Program Focal Person to maintain disposal site records in the sanitary landfill.
observations.reason: Lack of manpower and budget constraints

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the RCA be fenced with a secured gate at the entrance to minimize illegal visitors or scavengers on the RCA. This will also help address the occupational health and safety issues by limiting access.
observations.reason: Phase 1 construction of Perimeter Fence was done.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD Focal Person to train personnel assigned to man VAW Help Desks in the barangays to capacitate them to fully address cases on women violations in a gender-responsive manner.
observations.reason: Not all VAW Help Desks in the barangays are manned by competent personnel

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD Focal Person to establish a GAD Monitoring and Evaluation System.
observations.reason: Lack of manpower and budget constraints

observations.recommendation:
1. Municipal Accountant to maintain subsidiary ledger card for each group of
PPEs;
2. Property custodian to record promptly each transaction and update its
property cards as prescribed in the GAM for LGUs; and
3. Inventory Committee to prepare RPCPPE with complete data for each item,
as basis for reconciliation with the SLCs and PCs. Also, furnish the Audit
Team a copy of the duly accomplished RPCPPE not later than January 31
of each year in accordance with the GAM for LGUs.

1. Direct the Municipal Accountant and Supply Officer to: a) maintain
Supplies Ledger Cards and Stock Cards, respectively, complete with all the
required information; and b) reconcile their records periodically;
2. Require the inventory committee to prepare the RPCI with complete data
for each item, as basis for reconciliation with the SLCs and SCs. Also,
furnish the Audit Team a copy of the duly accomplished RPCI not later
than January 31 of each year in accordance with the GAM for LGUs;
3. Direct the Municipal Accountant to follow the proper accounting
procedures for inventories as prescribed in the GAM for LGUs; and
4. Prepare Adjusting Journal Entries to record the inventories found on hand
to present fairly the Financial Statements of the Municipality.

a. Prepare and certify a monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of
LDRRMF;
b. Verify the variances between the Report on the Utilization of DRRMF and
the amount presented in the Notes to Financial Statements and to make the
necessary corrections in the balances; and
c. Prepare the necessary adjusting entries to correct the balances of the Trust
Liabilities-DRRMF and Government Equity in the General Fund.

1. The Municipal Accountant to prepare Adjusting Journal Entries (AJE) to
record the receivables as follows:
Accounts Receivable 1,087,303.00
Prior Period Adjustment 1,087,303.00
Prior Period Adjustment 1,087,303.00
Government Equity 1,087,303.00
2. The Municipal Treasurer to demand payment from PDH for the unpaid
amount of the Waterworks System Fees and ensure to collect the exact
amount of monthly billings for the ensuing year; and
3. For the succeeding years, the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to furnish the
Accounting Section a copy of the Monthly Statement of Account so as the
Accountant can record the transactions accordingly.

1. Supply and/or Property Officer to fill out the date and other necessary
details in the Purchase Order;
2. Supply and/or Property officer and Inspecting Officer to fill out the date in
the Acceptance and Inspection Report to assure that the items received are
in accordance with the contract; and
3. Municipal Accountant to ensure completeness of supporting documents
for all disbursement transactions so as to ascertain the propriety of the
transactions.

We recommended that local government officials concerned to read and follow
guidelines that are created/issued for the implementation of the ESP thereby,
enabling the measures adopted to respond to the crisis brought by the COVID-
19 pandemic and become effective and efficient.

We recommended that the officials concerned to read and follow the guidelines
created for the proper implementation of the selection, utilization and/or paying
of the cash assistance of the program.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Budget
Officer, Municipal Planning and Development Officer, and Municipal
Engineer to properly optimize the utilization of the 20% Development Fund
allocated for the implementation of various Programs/Projects/Activities to
help achieve desirable socio-economic development and environmental
outcomes.
We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the balances of the book with the bank and prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries to correct the error.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal Treasurer to submit the afore-cited 46 cancelled checks for validation, and, control mechanism should design a control mechanism in the preparation of monthly reports including Report of Checks Issued.
We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal Treasurer to discontinue the collection of proceeds from the Eco-Tourism activity other than what were stated in the MOA.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to direct the officials concerned to stop the grant of the Cash Advances in compliance with provisions of Sections 4.1.4 to 4.1.5 and 4.3.1 of COA Circular No. 97-002, dated February 10, 1997.
We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal Budget Officer to properly classify expenditures under social and economic development funds in order for funds to be properly utilized.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Treasurer takes action on the imposed updates in local tax laws in order to increase the collection of revenues and taxes of the municipality.
We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to direct the Human Resource Management Officer to adhere to the guidelines set forth in hiring J.O. workers.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal officials concerned to make concerted efforts in acquiring the certificate of title so that the costs of infrastructure projects will not be subjected to suspension for lack of evidence of the right to possess.
We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Accountant to close the CIP account to the appropriated asset account upon completion of the project.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor compel the Municipal Accountant to strictly adhere to the practice of checking the completeness of documentations before claims are to be paid. Likewise, suppliers of goods must be required to submit required documentations as mandated under Section 9.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 to ensure that procured items are from legitimate sources.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Assessor to expedite the processing of documents for the titling of lands owned by the Municipality. We further recommended that before any construction is made, land ownership is established thru a certificate of title.

We recommended that a mechanism to measure the volume of waste and type of waste should be placed at the site, which will provide baseline data to assist in developing effective waste minimization strategies and future plans for waste management.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal Accountant
to strictly comply with the provisions of Section 74 of PD No. 1445; to regularly
prepare the BRS in reconciling with the cash in bank balances as recorded in
the books of accounts in comparison with the bank records, and consequently
make the necessary adjustments where appropriate. For the bank reconciling
items, the Municipal Accountant must make a representations with the bank to
effect whatever adjustments needed.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Accountant
and Municipal Treasurer to prepare written guidelines prior to the inventory
taking with clear instructions that all tangible assets are to be counted and
define roles of the members of the Inventory Committee to ensure the timely
completion and submission of the RPCPPE.

We further recommended that members of the Inventory Committee shall
undergo a training on Property and Supply Management System (PSMS), to
gain adequate understanding and working knowledge of the systems,
processes, policies and other regulations pertaining to property and supply
management.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Treasurer
to turn over the forty six issued and cancelled checks for validation and
consequently thereafter submit the checks issued cancelled or not together
with the RCI every end of the month or five days from the ensuing month for
the post-audit of said disbursements, and, that the Municipal Accountant
formulate stringent measures to adopt sound control over cash management
especially on the monthly rendering of the Report of Checks Issued.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Treasurer
to stop the collection of proceeds from the Eco-Tourism activity other than
what were stated in the MOA.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to direct the officials concerned to
discontinue the grant of the Cash Advances in compliance with provisions of
Sections 4.1.4 to 4.1.5 and 4.3.1 of COA Circular No. 97-002, dated February
10, 1997.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Accountant
to:
For the current year collectibles-
-to send within ten days before the expiration of the prescribed period as
written reminder under signature of the Mayor or his duly authorized
representative enjoining officials and employees concerned to settle their
cash advance;
-not to sign claims for additional cash advance until the previous cash
advance is settled;
-cause immediate withholding of salaries and claims of officers and
employees who failed to settle their cash advances within the prescribe
period after being sent notice and demand letter;
-furnish all department/ division heads listings of all unliquidated cash
advances of employees under their charge to enhance efforts to effect
liquidation.

For three to more than 5 years outstanding unliquidated cash advances-
-exhaust means to cause the effect of collection of the receivables
-make dialogues with the heirs of deceased accountable officers for possible
out of court settlements
-find the accountable officers whose addresses longer speak of their
current locations by using the social media and/or imposing strategy like
connecting with the closest kinship or friends.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal Accountant to
appropriately disclose in the financial statements the details of the loan of the
LGU and in so doing, pertinent documents relative to the loan availed of by
the Municipality should be strictly kept in file for future reference.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Accountant
to make the necessary disclosure pertaining to the dormant balances of the
account Due from NGOs and other LGUs and demand from NGOs and LGUs
through written communications, the immediate liquidation of projects granted
to them.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal Accountant
to request for write-off of the dormant account in accordance with COA
Circular No. 2016-005 together with an aging of the dormant receivables and
fund transfers, indicating on the remarks column the above-mentioned
applicable conditions.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to direct Municipal Budget Officer to
adhere to DBM-DILG JMC 2017-01 and properly classify expenditures under
social and economic development funds in order for funds to be utilized in
accordance with regulations.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor who is at the same time the HOPE
of the local government, together with the officials concerned, to be sensitive
in addressing the needs of constituents. Delivery of socio-economic welfare
is a top priority concern that should never be discounted of a fact such as
proclamation of election bans. This political intervention should not deter the
implementation of projects addressed to this need.

The BAC should implement strategic plans in the carrying out of all priority
projects because it is their responsibility to attend to the procurement needs
of the LGU.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to direct the LDRRMO to
implement the programs in the LDRRMP and the Local Disaster Risk
Reduction Management Council to monitor the utilization of the LDRRMF in
order to efficiently manage all types of emergencies and achieve orderly
transition from response to sustained recovery.

The BAC should implement strategic plans in the carrying out of all priority
projects because it is their responsibility to attend to the procurement needs
of the LGU.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor and the local sanggunian to :
1. craft ordinances and issuing executive orders related to waste
management and environmental protection;
2. strengthen the current waste management system of the Municipality,
including, garbage classification, collection, and recycling;
3. add more waste recycling facilities; and,
4. focus on the recycling methods of the recyclables.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Treasurer
to take action in imposing the updates on the assessment of Local Business
Tax (LBT), Registration and Renewal of Business Permits and Licenses, and
the imposition of Local Taxes, Fees and Charges in order to increase the
collection of revenues and taxes of the municipality.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to direct the Human Resource
Management Officer to adhere to the guidelines set forth in Section 77 of
R.A. No. 7160.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to direct the GAD FPS to
adhere to the provisions of JMC Circular Nos. 2013-01 and 2016-01
in the application of cost attribution of the annual expenditure to the
annual GADPB, in order to address efficiently and effectively the
streamlined gender issues of the locality.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal Accountant
to make the necessary disclosure on the reclassification made with the debit
balances of the account Due to NGA-BIR.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal Accountant
make the necessary disclosure with the dormant balances of the account.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Assessor
to facilitate the processing of land titles of those lots in various barangays
wherein structures were build.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal
Accountant to make an AJE in order to close the CIP account to the
appropriate asset account upon completion of the project.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Health
Officer to adhere to the provisions of the DOH Administrative Order No.
2018-001 dated January 28, 2018.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal Accountant
to enforce to persons liable stringent measures for the immediate settlement
of the issued disallowances.

1. Compel the Municipal Accountant to review the details of the Due from NGAs, Due from NGOs/POs and Other Receivable accounts and exert all efforts to locate records to prove the validity of claim from identified recipients of these funds; 2. Direct the Municipal Accountant to comply with the reportorial requirements on funds transferred to local or national government agencies or other debtors in order to avoid accumulation of unliquidated balances of the accounts.
observations.managementAction: Not implemented
observations.reason: Due to non-availability of documents to support claims.

observations.recommendation: that the Local Chief Executive should consider creating an organizing an Internal Audit Services, in compliance with R.A. 4177 and AO for a sound and adequate internal control system in the operations of the municipality.
observations.managementAction: Not acted by the management.
observations.reason: No action taken due to lack of fund resources and management.

observations.recommendation:
1.6 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor cause the creation of an
Inventory Committee to complete the conduct of physical count of all
properties of the Municipality and come up with the RPCPPE.
1.7 We further recommended that whoever is appointed to perform physical count
shall undergo a training on Property and Supply Management System (PSMS),
to gain adequate understanding and working knowledge of the systems,
processes, policies and other regulations pertaining to property and supply
management.

2.7 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to
immediately prepare Bank Reconciliation Statements on all bank accounts of the
Municipality and reconcile balances with the bank.
2.8 Management is amendable to the above-mentioned recommendation.

3.5 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant
to adopt stern regulations in the pursuit of zeroing-in the due and demandable
cash advances.

4.5 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Bid and Awards
Committee to stop splitting in regard to procurement of goods and infrastructures.
The Municipal Accountant shall likewise see to it that transactions of these type
shall not be repeated again. Provisions of Republic Act No. 9184 should be
adhered to by the BAC.
4.6 Nevertheless, officials concerned are required to render their explanations why
they resorted to splitting.

5.5 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor compel the Municipal Accountant to
strictly adhere to the practice of checking the completeness of documentations
before claims are to be paid. Said measure must be carried out to avoid
suspension or disallowance of transactions during post-audit.
5.6 Likewise, suppliers of goods must be required to submit required documentations
as mandated under Section 9.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 to ensure that
procured items are from legitimate sources.

6.5 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor compel the Municipal Accountant to
strictly adhere to the practice of checking the completeness of documentations
before claims are to be paid. Said measure must be carried out to avoid
suspension or disallowance of transactions during post-audit. Likewise, suppliers
of goods must be required to submit required documentations as mandated under
Section 9.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 to ensure that procured items are from
legitimate sources.

7.6 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Assessor to
expedite the processing of documents for the titling of lands owned by the
Municipality. We further recommended that before any construction is made, land
ownership thru a certificate of title is obtained.

8.10 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the SWM Focal Person to
consider the appropriate means of communication depending on the information
that it wishes to pass on to the public and its stakeholders. For instance, there
should be public notices on television and on the radio about who can access the
dumpsite, the working hours at the area to limit after-hours access and the
schedule for collection of wastes from households for different zones. Websites
have become increasingly effective and economical in disseminating
information. Therefore, it is important to have a specific link for SWM within
the Municipality’s website so that it is easier for the public to have access to
information and policy on solid waste management as well as any issue
concerning SWM.
8.11 To ensure effective implementation of the solid waste management plan, a well-
designed, current and targeted community awareness program is required. The
targeted audience should be considered when determining how the information
should be given to the public. The urgency of the matter should also be
considered so that everyone is aware as soon as possible.

9.4 We recommended that a mechanism to measure the volume of waste and type of
waste should be placed at the site, which will provide baseline data to assist in
developing effective waste minimization strategies and future plans for waste
management.

10.3 We recommended that a proper gate at the entrance to the RCA be built so that
it is easier for the personnel at the RCA to stop vehicles coming to the gate,
inspect the waste to confirm the type of waste to be disposed then direct the
vehicle to the designated area depending on the type of waste already inspected.
10.4 We further recommended that the SWM Focal Person should implement a color
coding or appropriate means of identification to ensure that wastes are properly
segregated at the RCA.

11.4 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the SWM Focal Person to
maintain disposal records in the RCA.

12.3 We recommended that the landfill be fenced with a secured gate at the
entrance to minimize illegal visitors or scavengers. This will also help address
the occupational health and safety issues by limiting access.

13.8 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Human Resource
Management Officer to adhere to the provisions set forth in Section 77 of RA
No. 7160.

14.5 We recommended that personnel assigned to man the VAW Help Desks in the
barangays be made to attend trainings to capacitate them to handle cases of
women violations.

15.6 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD Focal Person to
establish a GAD Monitoring and Evaluation System.

16.4 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant
to make reconciliation of their records against that of the GSIS. Furthermore,
the municipal accountant shall also verify who among the employees is
deficient in paying its loan obligations so that an adjustment in his/her
deduction shall be made.
16.5 Management adheres to the recommendation.

17.6 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Accountant to exert effort in enforcing the immediate settlement of audit
disallowances and, ensure that all employees who are retiring or transferring
to other agencies shall first settle the disallowances and charges for which
they are liable in accordance with Section 7 of COA Circular No. 2009-006
dated September 15, 2009.
17.7 Management adheres to the recommendation.

a) Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the required BRS; b) Make written representations with the depository banks to ensure prompt submission of the Bank Statements; c) Compel the Municipal Accountant to maintain an updated subsidiary ledger which will show the details or breakdown of the balance of the controlling account appearing in the general ledger.
observations.managementAction: Submitted BRSs only the Bank Office.
observations.reason: Partially implemented

observations.recommendation: that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant-designate to revert back the outstanding checks debited to Cash in Bank and credited to prior year’s adjustment duly supported with necessary required documents.
observations.managementAction: Submitted BRSs to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: 1. Direct the Inventory Committee to: a. Conduct complete physical count of properties; b. Submit to the Auditor the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RCPPE) duly reconciled with the accounting records; c. List all Property, Plant and Equipment found on hand, classifying them into serviceable or unserviceable; d. Maintain updated Property Cards per category on all property, plant and equipment of accounting records; e. Reconcile property records ensuring that the balances shown in the prepared RCPPE agree with the supply records and the book balances in the accounting records, and vice versa; f. Renew and maintain all Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment (ARE) for the signature of the holder of each item listed in the prepared RCPPE. 2. Direct the Municipal Assessor to produce a listing of all the properties owned by the government and reconcile them with those properties counted by the committee with an explanation for those items that are recorded in the books but could not be physically located. 3. Direct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to determine the validity of PPE account through reconciliation of the accounting records and the actual inventory. 4. Require the Municipal Accountant to: a. Prepare the Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards (PPELC) for each class of property, plant and equipment listing therein the data as to its acquisition, description, custody, estimated life, rate of depreciation, disposal, and other information about the property, plant and equipment, based on the source documents of the transactions; b. Drop from the books of accounts the unserviceable properties after the Waste Material Report and the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property are duly approved and evaluated by the COA.
observations.managementAction: Inventory List already submitted to the Inventory department but remained unreliable after the year-end of 2018.

observations.recommendation: a. Require the Municipal Accountant to send within ten (10) days before the expiration of the prescribed period a written reminder under signature of the LCE or his duly authorized representative, enjoining officials and employees concerned to settle their cash advance; b. Cause the immediate withholding of salaries and claims of officers and employees who failed to settle their cash advances within the prescribed period pursuant to Section 5.1.3. and 5.9 of COA Circular No. 97-002 after being sent notice and demand. The Municipal Accountant is authorized to delete the name of the official and/or employee from the subsequent payroll until such time that the cash advance has been fully liquidated pursuant to COA Circular 96-004.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant sent demand letters to Persons’ liable only in the government service.
observations.reason: The Municipal management will no longer allow the employee if he/she has still outstanding balances through cash or payroll deduction.

observations.recommendation: that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Assessor, Municipal Engineer, Municipal Agricultural Officer and Municipal Health Officer to fast-track the implementation of all the BUB projects.
observations.managementAction: management’s projects, some projects are now in the process for implementation and were already realigned as supplemental fund.
observations.reason: some projects implemented due to the reason that the MDRRO got sick in CY 2018 and she had undergone kidney transplant as revealed by the Municipal Accountant.

observations.recommendation: that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Budget Officer to strictly adhere to Section 287 of RA 7160 and joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-01 dated February 22, 2017 requiring the LGU to utilize the 20% component of the Internal Revenue Allotment (IRA) for the development social projects and environmental management.
observations.managementAction: In 2018, the salaries of the garbage collectors were charged against the MOOE of the 20% DF. But after the exit conference, the said salaries were charged against the General Fund of the Municipal Engineer’s Office.

observations.recommendation: a. Require the Municipal Risk Management Council to maximize the utilization of the MDRRMF in order to achieve the objectives of the fund and ensure readiness of the LGU in time of calamity; b. Compel the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Officer to conduct a case study for financial assistance extended to victims of calamity.
observations.managementAction: Projects under MDRRMO to maximize the utilization of the Realigned fund.

observations.recommendation: that the Local Chief Executive compel the Municipal Social Work Development Officer to fast tract the implementation of LCPC plans & programs.
observations.managementAction: MWDO is implementing the LCPC plans and programs.
observations.reason: MWDO is now undergoing implementation.

observations.recommendation: that the Local Chief Executive require the Local School Board to strictly observe the provision of DepEd, DBM, DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017 on Special Education Fund and direct the Municipal Treasurer to submit liquidations of cash advances made out of the SEF regulation.
observations.managementAction: After the exit conference in CY 2018, the Local School Board adheres to DepEd, DBM, DILG Joint Circular No. 1 and follow to disburse on expenditures authorized and to be charged against the SEF.

observations.recommendation: that the Local Chief Executive direct: 1. The Human Resource Management Officer to review the duties and functions of the Job Order Workers so as not to duplicate the duties and functions of the regular plantilla employees: 2. The Municipal Budget Officer to adjust the rates of Job Order Workers in compliance to regulations.
observations.managementAction: As revealed by the Municipal Accountant, management had already given an increase of salaries to all Job Order employees as stipulated in the DOLE Wage Order.

observations.recommendation: 1. Require the Municipal remits the same to GSIS as reflected in bills. 2. Require the Municipal Treasurer that the amount withheld shall be remitted to GSIS in full within 10 days following the end of the month the withholding was made or the withholding was accrued; 3. Require the concerned employee in-charge in the preparation of GSIS remittances and municipal accountant to conduct thorough reconciliation with its records with that of the GSIS and give priority to those affected employees with negative balances for one (1) month to three (3) years, and request from GSIS for refund; 4. Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare a subsidiary ledger for Due to GSIS account.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant instructed the personnel in-charge in the preparation of the remittances to GSIS to strictly adhere to the provisions of RA 8291 so that failures and lapses will be avoided.
observations.reason: The Municipal Treasurer remits the amounts to GSIS but not all discrepancies resolved; Municipal Accountant to prepare subsidiary ledger.

observations.recommendation: that the Municipal Mayor compel the LGU’s Solid Waste Management Board to strictly implement feasible re-use, recycling, and composting programs and other prescribed procedures of RA No. 9003. Also, implement the projects, programs and activities listed in the AIP especially those related to effective solid waste management. We also recommended that the Mayor provide the necessary financial and technical support in enjoining the barangays to establish their own Materials Recovery Facility according to standards and strictly implement waste segregation.
observations.managementAction: The Local Government has implemented some projects and activities in some selected barangays; in the town proper some households are connected to the LGU system and the LGU collects garbage only in the town proper facility.
observations.reason: MRF implemented in some selected barangays only; not in all barangays; more technical and financial support needed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Water System Board to formulate strategies to increase collections and to expand distribution lines and increase targeted households to increase LGU’s income.
observations.managementAction: The Local Government already implemented strategies to increase income from the operation of its water system and expansion of water distribution lines to targeted households to increase LGU’s income.
observations.reason: Not all households connected; expansion ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive remind the concerned officials to refrain from claiming full transportation allowances if they are actually using government vehicle during travel.
observations.managementAction: Transportation Allowance are already deducted from department heads if he/she was using government vehicle during travel.

observations.recommendation: that the Local Chief Executive compel the GAD Council to prioritize the implementation of the CBMS for the creation of GAD database to serve as inputs or basis for planning, programming and policy formulation.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal GAD Focal Point prepared Local GAD Code.

observations.recommendation: that the Local Chief Executive require the BAC to strictly adhere to Section 37.4.2 of RA 9184, i.e. to post Notices of Awards, Notices to Proceed and Approved Contracts of Agreements for transparency and information in all procurement processes.
observations.managementAction: The BAC posted almost all of the Notices of Awards, Notices to Proceed and Approved Contracts of Agreement for transparency and information in all procurement processes.
observations.reason: Internet Connection is not always available; some documents were submitted late.

observations.recommendation: that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to regularly reconcile the amounts withheld and remitted to Pag-IBIG in order to ascertain the accuracy and reliability of the balances of Due to Pag-IBIG account. A subsidiary ledger must be maintained for each employee’s Pag-IBIG contributions/loan amortizations.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant revealed.
observations.reason: There is a balance from previous years which is still unreconciled due to absence of available documents turned over to the Municipal Accountant.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Chief Executive direct the Municipal Agriculturist to submit to the Accounting Section any addition or disposal of the breeding stocks being monitored for recording. Direct also the Municipal Accountant to verify the unreconciled balance of the breeding stocks and make necessary adjustment entries.
observations.managementAction: Not implemented by the Inventory Committee of the LGU as revealed by the Municipal Accountant because they prioritized the implementation of some BUB projects during the passed years of 2017 & 2018.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive enjoin the Sangguniang Bayan through the Committee on the Women and Family to spearhead the formulation of the Local GAD Code and to prepare the GAD Accomplishment Report together with the attachments and submit it to COA in accordance with the provisions set forth under COA Circular No. 2014-0011 dated March 18, 2014 and PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2013-01 as well as the formulation of Local GAD Code or Ordinance.
observations.managementAction: The Local GAD Code was prepared by the GAD Focal Point of the LGU.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to strictly comply with the provision of COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015; Implement necessary adjustments and reclassify the said disbursements to appropriate accounts.
observations.managementAction: There are already ledgers recorded in the PPE Accounts and the Municipal Accountant already recorded all disbursements to other structures on the PPE accounts.

observations.recommendation: that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain Subsidiary Ledgers for these liabilities and also to prepare the Schedules that support each liability account.
observations.managementAction: There are ledgers for these liabilities but these ledgers are still not yet supported with schedules of each liability account.

observations.recommendation: that the Local Chief Executive direct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to submit perfected contract and purchase orders with its supporting documents to COA within the prescribed period.
observations.managementAction: The BAC submitted only some required supporting documents to COA audit team.
observations.reason: Based on the dated received of transmittals, some of the perfected contracts were submitted late.

observations.recommendation: that the Local Chief Executive direct the Accountant to strictly observe the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 and intensify the liquidation of outstanding cash advances by sending demand letters to persons liable.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant sent demand letters but not all responded and some difficulties found in sending those who are no longer in government service.

observations.recommendation: that the LCE exert efforts to facilitate the titling of the lots in the name of the Municipality as ownership over the properties to avoid disallowance thereof. We also recommend that the Municipal Accountant make proper disclosures in the Notes to Financial Statements on the actual status of the land.
observations.managementAction: Titling is still in process according to the Municipal Assessor-Designate.

observations.recommendation: that the Local Chief Executive cause the provision of financial and technical assistance to the barangays for the establishment and operations of functional and standard-based MRFs and to implement programs for source reduction, recycling and composting.
observations.managementAction: The MENRO-Designate is implementing some MRF projects in some selected barangays only. The LGU prioritized some barangays and MRFs due to limited resources.

observations.recommendation: that the Local Chief Executive direct the BAC to submit to COA the supporting documents of the 16 contracts and to adhere to the provisions of the Revised IRR of RA 9184 and COA Circular No. 2009-0001 dated February 12, 2009.
observations.managementAction: The BAC submitted only few required documents to the Audit Team.
observations.reason: Some required documents not submitted due to internet connectivity and late submissions.

observations.recommendation: that the Local Chief Executive enjoin the Sangguniang Bayan through the Committee on Women and Family to spearhead the formulation of the Local GAD Code and to facilitate understanding and application of the guidelines. Also recommended that the municipality through its GAD Focal Point System prepare the GAD Accomplishment Report together with its attachments and submit it to COA in accordance with COA Circular No. 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014 and PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2013-01.
observations.reason: The management did not prepare the Local GAD code or submit the GAD Accomplishment Report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
a) Direct the Municipal Accountant to immediately prepare the required BRS;
b) Make written representations with the depository banks to ensure prompt submission
of the Bank statements;
c) Compel the Municipal Accountant to maintain an updated subsidiary ledger which will
show the details or breakdown of the balance of the controlling account appearing in
the general ledger.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Accountant-designate to revert back the outstanding checks ranging from two (2) years
to five (5) years in the books as debit to cash in bank and credit to prior year’s
adjustment duly supported with necessary required documents.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
1. Direct the Municipal Inventory Committee to:
a. conduct complete physical count of Property, Plant and equipment;
b. submit to the Auditor the Report on the Physical Count of Property,
Plant and Equipment (RCPPE) duly reconciled with the accounting
records;
c. list all property, Plant and Equipment found on hand, classifying them
into serviceable or unserviceable;
d. maintain updated property cards per category on all property, plant and
Equipment of accounting records;
e. reconcile property records enduring that the balances shown in the
prepared RCPPE agree with the supply records and the book balances
in the accounting records, and vice versa;
f. renew and maintain all acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment (ARE)
for the signature of the holder of each item listed in the prepared
RCPPE;
g. prepare the necessary Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable
Property and Waste Materials Report for the unserviceable properties
found on hand, taking into consideration EO No. 888 March 1983, COA
Memorandum No. 2005-027.
2. Direct the Municipal Assessor to produce a listing of all the properties
owned by the government and reconcile them with those properties counted
by the committee with an explanation for those items that are recorded un
the books but could not be physically located;
3. Direct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to determine
the validity of PPE account through reconciliation of the accounting records
and the actual inventory;
4. Require the Municipal Accountant to:
a. Prepare the Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards (PPELC) for
each class of property, plant and equipment listing therein the data as to
its acquisition, description, custody, estimated life, rate of depreciation,
disposal, and other information about the property, plant and equipment,
based on the source documents of the transactions;
b. Drop from the books of accounts the unserviceable properties after the
Waste Material Report and the Inventory and Inspection Report of
Unserviceable Property are duly approved and evaluated by the COA.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Engineer,
Municipal Assessor, Municipal Agricultural Officer and Municipal Health Officer to
fast-track the implementation of priority programs, projects and activities funded by the
BUB fund and LGSF to provide constituents of the benefits due them.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Budget
Officer to strictly adhere to Section 287 of RA 7160 and Joint Memorandum Circular
No. 1 s. 2017 dated February 22, 2017, requiring the LGU to utilize the 20% component
of the Internal Revenue Allotment (IRA) for development projects that will contribute
to the attainment of a desirable socio-economic development and environmental
management outcomes and make sure to utilize the fund for capital expenditures or in
the nature of investment.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive:
a. Require the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Council to maximize
the utilization of the MDRRMF in order to achieve the objectives of the fund and
will ensure readiness of the LGU in times of calamity;
b. Compel the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Officer to conduct a
case study for financial assistance extended to victims of calamity.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive compel the Municipal Social
Work Development Officer to fast track the implementation of LCPC plans &
programs.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Local School
Board to strictly observe the provision of DepEd, DBM, DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s.
2017 on Special Education Fund and direct the Municipal Treasurer to submit
liquidations of cash advances made out of the SEF.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct:
1. the Human Resource Management Officer to review the duties and functions of the
Job Order Workers so as not to duplicate the duties and functions of the regular
plantilla employees;
2. the Municipal Budget Officer to adjust the rates of Job Order Workers in
compliance to regulations.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
1. Require the Municipal Accountant to instruct the personnel in-charge in the
preparation of the remittance of employees and employer due to GSIS to
adhere strictly to the provisions of RA 8291 so that failures and lapses will be
avoided.
2. Require the Municipal Treasurer that the amount withheld shall be remitted to GSIS
in full within 10 days following the end of the month the withholding was made or the
withholding was accrued.
3. Require the concerned employee in-charge in the preparation of GSIS remittances and
municipal accountant to conduct thorough reconciliation with its records with that of
the GSIS and give priority to those affected employees with negative balances for one
(1) month to three (3) years, and request from GSIS for refund.
4. Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare a subsidiary ledger for Due to GSIS
account.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor compel the Municipal Solid Waste
Management Board to strictly implement feasible re-use, recycling, and composting
programs and other prescribed procedures of RA No. 9003. Also, implement the
projects, programs and activities listed in the AIP especially information dissemination
campaign to adopt measures toward effective solid waste management at the barangay
level.

We also recommended that the Mayor provide the necessary financial and
technical support in enjoining the barangays to establish their own Materials Recovery
Facility according to standards and strictly implement waste segregation.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
1. Compel the Municipal Accountant to review the details of the Due from
NGAs, Due from NGOs/POs and Other Receivable accounts and exert all
efforts to locate records to prove the validity of claim from the identified
recipients of these funds.
2. Direct the Municipal Accountant to comply with the reportorial requirements
on funds transferred to local or national government agencies or and other
debtors in order to avoid accumulation of unliquidated balances of the
accounts.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Water System
Policy Board to formulate strategies to increase its income from operation of its water
system.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive remind concerned officials to
refrain from claiming full transportation allowances if they are actually using
government vehicle. Full adherence to Section 56 of the General Appropriation Act of
2015 and COA Circular No. 2000-005

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive compel the GAD Council to
prioritize the implementation of the CBMS for the creation of GAD database to serve
as inputs or basis for planning, programming and policy formulation.

The Local Chief Executive should consider creating and organizing an Internal
Audit Services, in compliance with R.A. No. 4177 and AO 70 to give assurance for a
sound and adequate internal control system in the operations of the Municipality.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive require the BAC to strictly
adhere to Section 37.4.2 of RA 9184, i.e. to post in the PHilGEPS Notices of Awards,
Notice to Proceed, and Approved Contract of Agreement for transparency and
information in all procurement processes.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Accountant to regularly reconcile the amounts withheld and remitted to Pag-IBIG in
order to ascertain the accuracy and reliability of the balances of Due to Pag-IBIG
account in the financial statements. A subsidiary ledger must be maintained for each
employee’s Pag-IBIG contributions / loan amortizations.
that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to strictly comply with the provision of COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015 and make the necessary adjustment.
observations.managementAction: Some disbursements recorded in the PPE disbursements
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant still recorded some disbursements to other structures under the Property, Plant and Equipment accounts.

observations.recommendation: that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustments and henceforth adhere to the provisions stated in Chapter 17 of the Government Accounting Manual Volume 1.

that the Local Chief Agriculturist to submit to the Accounting Office any additions or disposal of the breeding stocks being monitored for recording. Direct also, the Municipal Accountant to verify the unreconciled balance of the breeding stocks and make necessary adjusting entries.
observations.managementAction: None
observations.reason: No report received by the Accounting Office regarding any addition or disposal of the breeding stocks

observations.recommendation: that the Local Chief Accountant prepare and maintain the Subsidiary Ledgers and also to prepare the Schedules that support each liability account.
observations.reason: Subsidiary Ledgers are not yet updated by the Office of the Municipal Accountant.

observations.recommendation: that the Local Chief Executive cause the creation of an inventory committee who shall conduct physical count of all its inventories.

that the Local Chief Executive direct the Bids and Award Committee (BAC) to submit perfected contract and Purchase Orders with its supporting documents to COA within the prescribed period as mandated in COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.
observations.reason: Not all perfected contracts and Purchase Orders are submitted to COA.

observations.recommendation: that the Local Chief Executive cause the preparation of Memorandum of Agreement with the contracting party to include a provision specifying the duties and responsibilities of the funding agency, the municipality and the third-party beneficiaries. The MOA must also include a work and financial plan that would identify and address all details of the project.
that the Local Chief Executive intensify their efforts in the liquidation of outstanding cash advances by sending demand letters to persons liable.
observations.reason: Despite the sending of demand letters to persons liable, not all responded positively; and we found difficulty in sending to those who are not anymore.

observations.recommendation: that the concerned officials include in the LDRRMF the unutilized portion of the 70% MOOE and 30% QRF of prior years as stated in Section 21 of Republic Act No. 10121 dated May 27, 2010 and COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

that the LCE exert efforts to facilitate the titling of the lots in the name of the Municipality as proof of ownership of the Municipality. We also recommend that the Municipal Accountant make proper disclosures in the Notes to Financial Statements on the actual status of the land.
observations.reason: The Municipal Assessor-Designate has taken action on the titling of the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: that the Local Chief Executive, being the chair of the Local School Board officials strictly observe the provisions of Section 272 of RA 7160 and advise the administrators in their respective locality to revise their School Improvement Plan to include the expenses enumerated under DepEd Order No. 12, s. 2012.
that the local Chief Executive cause the provision of financial and technical assistance to the barangays for the establishment and operations of functional and standard-based MRFs.
observations.reason: The MENRO had already conducted monitoring on some MRF to cause the provision of financial and technical assistance to the barangays. However, the establishment and operations of functional and standard-based MRFs remain incomplete.

observations.recommendation: that the Local Chief Executive cause the provision of financial and technical assistance to the barangays for the establishment and operations of functional and standard-based MRFs and intensify information dissemination programs.
observations.reason: The MENRO had already conducted monitoring on some MRF to cause the barangays to establish functional MRFs.

observations.recommendation: that the Local Chief Executive direct the BAC to submit to COA supporting documents of the 16 contracts and to adhere to the provisions of the Revised IRR of RA 9184 and COA Circular No. 2009-0001 dated February 12, 2009.
observations.reason: Some projects were submitted but supporting documents required by COA were not all provided.

observations.recommendation: that the BAC discontinue the practice of delaying the procurement process and adhere strictly to Annex C of the RIRR which sets the minimum and maximum allowable time to act on various activities from posting/advertisement for issuance of Notice to Proceed.
observations.reason: Some procurement activities were acted upon late causing delays in project implementation.

observations.recommendation: that the LCE direct all concerned officials of the LGU to be vigilant and strictly adhere to the provisions of National Budget Circular No. 446-A dated January 30, 1998 regarding procurement of second hand vehicles so as to avoid wastage of government funds.
observations.reason: Lack of funds; management had already made some repairs to the particular equipment but full compliance with procurement guidance remains outstanding.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the accountant and the Municipal Engineer to:


        a. Strictly comply with  the  provision  under  PD  No.  1445;  COA
           Resolution No. 2014-003 dated January 4, 2014 and  COA  Circular
           No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015;


        b. Make the necessary adjustment:
                      Donations                                 950,000.00
                                            Other                Structures
                 950,000.00
                                   To   adjust   overstatement   of   Other
              Structure


        c. Make sure that all fund transfers be covered by a Memorandum  of
           Agreement to include a provision that the infrastructure project
           must be inspected by the Municipal Engineer upon completion.  An
           Inspection  Report  should  then  be  attached   to   the   fund
           utilization report.

Based on the aforementioned guidelines,  we  recommended  that  the
   Local Chief  Executive  direct  the  Municipal  Accountant  to  make  the
   necessary adjustments and henceforth adhere to the provisions  stated  in
   Chapter 17 of the Government Accounting Manual Volume 1.

We recommended  that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
   Agriculturist to submit  to  the  Accounting  Section  any  additions  or
   disposal of the breeding stocks being monitored  for  recording.   Direct
   also, the Municipal Accountant to verify the unreconciled balance of  the
   breeding stocks and make necessary adjusting entries. Henceforth,  adhere
   to the standards, policies, procedures and guidelines on the recognition,
   measurement, presentation and  disclosure  requirements  of  agricultural
   activity discussed in Chapter 11  of  the  Government  Accounting  Manual
   volume I.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the  Municipal
   Accountant to prepare and maintain the necessary Subsidiary  Ledgers  and
   also to prepare the schedules that support each liability account and  to
   adhere to the provisions of P.D. 1445 and Government Accounting Manual.

We recommended that the Local chief Executive direct the  officials
   concerned to:

          Cause a  creation  of  inventory  committee  who  shall  conduct
           physical count of all its inventories.
           Require  the  preparation  of  Report  on  Physical  Count   of
           Inventory, a copy of which to be submitted to the auditor within
           the reglementary period.
          Reconcile the results of the actual count  with  the  accounting
           records.
          Address the lack of manpower of the accounting section  so  that
           Supplies Ledger Card can be prepared.
          Refrain  from  assigning  multiple  functions  to  the  Property
           Custodian so she can focus on her duties and tasks as a Property
           Custodian  like the preparation of Stock Cards  and  conduct  of
           physical count of inventories.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the  Bids  and
   Award Committee (BAC) to submit perfected contract  and  Purchase  Orders
   (PO) with its supporting documents to COA within the prescribe period  as
   mandated in COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.

We recommended that the local Chief Executive direct the  Municipal
   Accountant to strictly observe the provisions of COA Circular  No.  2012-
   001 and that all funds received must have a Memorandum of Agreement  with
   the contracting party to include a provision specifying  the  duties  and
   responsibilities of the funding agency, the municipality and  the  third-
   party beneficiaries.  The MOA must also include a work and financial plan
   that would identify and address all details of the project.

We  recommended  that  the  Local  Chief  Executive    direct   the
   Accountant to strictly observe the provisions of COA Circular No.  97-002
   dated February 10, 1997 and intensify their efforts for  the  liquidation
   of outstanding cash advances by sending demand  letters,  otherwise,  the
   consequences provided under Circular 2012-004 dated November 28, 2012 may
   be imposed. For those accountable  officers  who  are  already  deceased,
   management should exert  effort  for  the  collections/liquidations  from
   their heirs, else, ask for write off of such  accounts.   For  those  not
   connected in LGU-Pintuyan anymore and  those  that  have  transferred  to
   another office, demand letters should be sent or else  seek  other  means
   for the liquidations and collections of  their  past  due  accounts.  For
   those negative accounts and telephone charges, the Local Chief  Executive
   should direct the Accountant to verify  the  said  accounts  and  prepare
   adjusting entries, if necessary, to correct the affected accounts.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the  concerned
   officials to include in the LDRRMFIP the unutilized portion  of  the  70%
   MOOE and 30% QRF of prior years as stated in Section 21 of  Republic  Act
   No. 10121 dated  May  27,  2010  and  COA  Circular  No.  2012-002  dated
   September 12, 2012.

We recommended that the LCE exert efforts to facilitate the titling
   of the lots in the name of the Municipality as proof  of  ownership  over
   the properties to avoid disallowance thereof.


        We also recommended  that  the  Municipal  Accountant  make  proper
   disclosures in the Notes to Financial Statements on the actual status  of
   the land.

We recommended  that the Local Chief Executive, being the chair  of
   the Local School Board strictly observe the provisions of Section 272  of
   RA 7160 and advise the school administrators in their respective locality
   to  revise  their  School  Improvement  Plan  to  include  the   expenses
   enumerated under DepEd Order No. 12, s. 2012.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive cause  the  provision
   of  financial  and  technical  assistance  to  the  barangays   for   the
   establishment and operations of functional and standard-based MRFs.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

        a. Cause the discontinuance of the  use  of  open  dump  sites  and
           identification of possible  sanitary  landfill  to  develop  and
           operate as a final disposal site for solid wastes. This landfill
           shall  be  designed  and  operated  in   accordance   with   the
           guidelines/criteria mentioned under RA 9003.


        a. Cause the strict implementation of the  policy  on  the  use  of
           personal protective gear by the garbage collectors.


        b. Direct the municipal official concerned to instill awareness  to
           the public on the No littering and No open burning  policies
           and   the   conduct    of    information/education/communication
           activities  pertaining  to  solid  waste  management  among  the
           constituents  through  several   strategies.   One   recommended
           strategy is  incorporating  issues  on  solid  waste  management
           during pre-marriage counseling conducted by the LGU. Another  is
           the inclusion of the subject during seminars  conducted  by  the
           Department  of  Health,  Department  of  Agriculture  and  other
           government line agencies.  Calling  the  attention  of  barangay
           officials and conducting  dialogues  on  the  issues  concerning
           solid waste management is also an offered strategy.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive  direct  the  BAC  to
   submit to COA supporting documents of the 16 contracts and to adhere  the
   provisions of the Revised IRR of Republic Act (RA) 9184 and COA  Circular
   No. COA Circular No. 2009-0001 dated February 12, 2009.

We recommended that the  BAC  shall  discontinue  the  practice  of
   delaying the procurement process and adhere strictly to Annex  C  of  the
   RIRR which sets the minimum and maximum allowable time to act on  various
   activities from posting /advertisement for issuance of NTP.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive enjoin the Sanggunian
   Bayan through the Committee on Women and Family to spearhead the
   formulation of the Local GAD Code and  to  facilitate  understanding  and
   application of the guidelines, the municipality may refer to the GAD Code
   Guidelines of the Philippine Commission of Women (PCW) for a step-by-step
   tutoring.


We recommended that the municipality through its GAD Focal Point System
   prepare  the  GAD  Accomplishment  Report  together  with  its  attachments
   and submit it to COA in accordance with the provisions set  forth  under
   COA Circular No. 2014-001 dated March 18,  2014  and  PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA
   Joint memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2013-01 as  well  as  the formulation
   of Local GAD Code or Ordinance.

We recommended that the LCE must direct all concerned officials  of
   the LGU to be vigilant and strictly adhere to the provisions of  National
   Budget Circular No. 446-A dated January 30, 1998 regarding procurement of
   second hand vehicles so as to avoid wastage of government funds.
